beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Our framework, termed Vision ARC (VARC), achieves 60.4% accuracy... competitive with those of leading LLMs

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Examples of unseen tasks solved by VARC. ... VARC correctly solves these challenging tasks.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: VARC achieves 54.5% accuracy on the ARC-1 benchmark, using a small model with only 18 million parameters.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: This result substantially surpasses the best recurrent methods [53, 27] that are also trained from scratch on ARC.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Combining VARC models through ensembling [29] further improves accuracy to 60.4%...

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: ...matching the reported average human performance [31] on the ARC-1 dataset.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Examples of unseen tasks solved by VARC. ... VARC correctly solves these challenging tasks.

beliefs.gloss: New demo pairs at inference enable test-time training.
beliefs.evidence: The bottom right shows a sequence of grids illustrating the test-time training process, where an initial incorrect prediction... gradually becomes more accurate...

beliefs.gloss: Test-time training adapts model to task-specific transformations.
beliefs.evidence: This visualization illustrates the model's behavior of adapting to task-specific transformations through few-shot test-time training.

beliefs.gloss: Larger models risk overfitting in the current setting.
beliefs.evidence: Going beyond this regime can lead to overfitting in our current setting, as shown in Tab. 1 for the 66M ViT model.

beliefs.gloss: Classical vision backbones can effectively address the ARC problem.
beliefs.evidence: all U-Net variants achieve decent accuracy, suggesting that this problem can also be effectively addressed by classical vision backbones.

beliefs.gloss: 2D positional embeddings preserve image structure, preventing loss from 1D sequences.
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial: see Fig. 7(b)(c).

beliefs.gloss: 2D positional embeddings preserve image structure, preventing loss from 1D sequences.
beliefs.evidence: we replace the 2D ROPE in Fig. 7(f) with a 1D ROPE and observe a degradation of 3.5 points

beliefs.gloss: 2D positional embeddings preserve image structure, preventing loss from 1D sequences.
beliefs.evidence: We empirically show that explicitly modeling positions in 2D is essential.

beliefs.gloss: 2D positional embeddings preserve image structure, preventing loss from 1D sequences.
beliefs.evidence: This 2D structure can be lost if we naïvely treat the embedded patches as a 1D sequence.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data space, reduces overfitting, and learns spatial priors.
beliefs.evidence: Even so, the 2×2 patchification leads to a noticeable gain of 2.4 points... which substantially enriches the data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas enables augmentations, fostering translation and scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: These priors jointly have a gain of 27.7 points, where the canvas-based designs (cf) has a gain of 11.5 points.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data space, reduces overfitting, and learns spatial priors.
beliefs.evidence: These priors jointly have a gain of 27.7 points, where the canvas-based designs (cf) has a gain of 11.5 points.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas enables augmentations, fostering translation and scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: In Fig. 7(e), we apply fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas. ... this setting yields an additional gain of 2.9 points...

beliefs.gloss: Patchification incorporates inductive biases: locality and translation invariance.
beliefs.evidence: In Fig. 7(e), we apply fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas. ... this setting yields an additional gain of 2.9 points...

beliefs.gloss: Canvas enables augmentations, fostering translation and scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: In Fig. 7(f), we further apply the scale augmentation enabled by the concept of canvas. Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain of 6.2 points.

beliefs.gloss: Offline training enables learning visual common sense.
beliefs.evidence: As expected, offline training greatly improves the performance of TTT, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned from the training set.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks are solvable without prior offline training.
beliefs.evidence: even without offline training, our TTT strategy can achieve nontrivial accuracy (26.4), suggesting that some tasks in this benchmark can be solved tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: Multi-view predictions are consolidated by majority voting for pass@2.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel errors render entire ARC predictions incorrect.
beliefs.evidence: in ARC, a mistake on even a single pixel renders the entire prediction incorrect.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel errors render entire ARC predictions incorrect.
beliefs.evidence: This may explain the large gain seen here.

beliefs.gloss: Multi-view predictions are consolidated by majority voting for pass@2.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Our VARC with 18M parameters is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, a >20% relative improvement.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Our VARC with 18M parameters is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, a >20% relative improvement.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Our VARC with 18M parameters is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, a >20% relative improvement.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: Following the classical ensembling practice in vision... Doing so boosts our result to 60.4.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: This result closes the gap with the reported average human performance (60.2 [31]).

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: VARC (ensemble, 73M) achieves 60.4 on ARC-1 and 11.1 on ARC-2.

beliefs.gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning, without language.
beliefs.evidence: The VARC ensemble result (60.4) closes the gap with average human performance (60.2).

beliefs.gloss: Attention maps reveal pixel-to-pixel reasoning for copying.
beliefs.evidence: These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel to copy from.

beliefs.gloss: Different layers specialize in focusing on distinct structures and contexts.
beliefs.evidence: Different layers exhibit different behavior.

beliefs.gloss: Task embedding visualization suggests learning relations between tasks.
beliefs.evidence: Interestingly, we observe that nearby points in the task embedding space exhibit similar semantics. ... This visualization suggests that our method attempts to learn the relations between different tasks...

beliefs.gloss: Increasing offline training data is beneficial, with diminishing returns.
beliefs.evidence: Increasing the amount of offline training data is beneficial, although even without it, our model can achieve decent accuracy.

beliefs.gloss: Diversity of training tasks improves generalization.
beliefs.evidence: Increasing task diversity is beneficial.

beliefs.gloss: Increasing offline training data is beneficial, with diminishing returns.
beliefs.evidence: Using only the original ARC training data, without any RE-ARC data, our method achieves a decent accuracy of 31.5.

beliefs.gloss: Increasing offline training data is beneficial, with diminishing returns.
beliefs.evidence: By adding 10, 100, and 1,000 pairs per task from RE-ARC, the accuracy increases to 38.6, 52.3, and 54.0, respectively.

beliefs.gloss: Increasing offline training data is beneficial, with diminishing returns.
beliefs.evidence: This comparison suggests that increasing the amount of offline training data is beneficial, although the returns diminish beyond a certain point.

beliefs.gloss: Model produces correct predictions in many views, but voting filters some.
beliefs.evidence: As expected, as the number of proposals (k) increases, the pass@k accuracy increases. ... This result indicates that our model produces correct predictions in some of the many views...

beliefs.gloss: Model produces correct predictions in many views, but voting filters some.
beliefs.evidence: This result indicates that our model produces correct predictions in some of the many views, although such correct cases are not sufficiently populated to be retained after voting.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks admit multiple plausible rules.
beliefs.evidence: some may admit multiple plausible explanations or rules.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks admit multiple plausible rules.
beliefs.evidence: our model attempts to interpret the rule as either 'going-through-only' (attempt 1) or 'touching' (attempt 2).

beliefs.gloss: Most ARC tasks are unambiguous.
beliefs.evidence: Although most ARC tasks are unambiguous, some may admit multiple plausible explanations or rules.

beliefs.evidence: As the test-time training progresses, the model's predictions gradually converge toward the correct output.
